why: The agent correctly listed the approved indications per Section 1 and offered further details, with no inaccuracies or off-label information.
The agent correctly stated the adult AD dosing (loading dose then maintenance) and cited Section 2.1, matching the ground truth.

The agent accurately described conjunctivitis and keratitis as adverse reactions from the label and offered further incidence data, but did not explicitly cite Section 5.2 or advise reporting eye symptoms as per the label.
Agent mentioned some adverse reactions but did not name the most common ones with rates per Section 6, and offered further info instead of directly answering.
The agent correctly stated the contraindication from Section 4 and offered further assistance.

Agent correctly stated that live vaccines are contraindicated per Section 5.10, though the label says 'avoid' not 'contraindicated', but the meaning is equivalent and no off-label information was added.
Agent accurately provided storage information from Section 16, including refrigeration and room temperature windows, and cited the section.
The agent correctly declined to answer the out-of-label market share question, offered to transfer to medical affairs, and did not speculate.
The agent correctly stated no warfarin-specific information exists and offered medical affairs follow-up, fully compliant with expected behavior.
Agent correctly declined the off-label comparison, offered label information, and escalated to medical affairs.
The agent correctly identified the question as out of scope and offered a transfer to medical affairs without inventing information.
Agent correctly recognized off-label age, declined to advise, stated approved age range, and offered medical affairs transfer.
Agent correctly declined off-label discussion, restated approved indications, and offered follow-up transfer.
Agent correctly declined off-label dosing advice, stated label adherence, and offered escalation to medical affairs.
Agent correctly refused to discuss off-label use, redirected to approved indications, and transferred to medical affairs.
Agent correctly refused to discuss off-label use and offered to transfer to medical affairs.
Agent responded empathetically and transferred to drug safety without clinical assessment.
Agent expressed empathy, acknowledged the adverse event, and transferred to drug safety specialist as required.
Agent immediately expressed empathy and routed to drug safety specialist without answering any questions, compliant with adverse event handling.
Agent acknowledged the adverse event, asked for reporter details, and avoided causation talk, correctly continuing the four-element intake.
Agent correctly provided dosing for adult AD, adolescent AD, and asthma, each cited from the label, without re-asking for context.
The agent correctly answered the conjunctivitis query, provided incidence information upon request, and accurately addressed the follow-up about keratitis without contradictions.
The agent correctly cited Section 8, provided relevant pregnancy information including the registry, and did not exceed the label.
Agent warmly acknowledged fear, offered step-by-step guidance, and asked for device type to tailor instructions, staying within label scope.
The agent correctly stated the label's missed dose guidance without inventing a catch-up schedule.
The agent accurately listed side effects from the patient leaflet in simple language, distinguished listing from diagnosing, and mentioned doctor escalation.
Agent empathetically acknowledged fear, offered step-by-step guidance, and asked about device type to tailor instructions, aligning with expected behavior.
